name: Johnnycake with Chervil
recipeInstructions:
If chervil is unavailable, Italian parsley can be used instead.
1. Brush a square 8-inch baking pan with the oil. Once the oven is preheated and the pan hot (but watch carefully), proceed.
2. Sift flour with sugar, baking powder, and salt. Stir in cornmeal. In a separate bowl, mix eggs with oil and buttermilk. Chop chervil leaves to yield about 1/4 cup. Stir into milk-egg mixture.
3. Make a well in the dry ingredients and pour in liquid quickly. Blend just until mixed; do not overmix.
4. Pour batter into the prepared pan. Bake at 425°F for 20 minutes, or until golden brown and crisp around edges (muffins or cornsticks may only take 15 minutes). Cut into squares and serve immediately, spreading with butter if desired.
5. Save any leftovers and freeze for cornbread poultry stuffing.
6. Enright suggests serving this with stews, chowders, fried chicken or ribs, and also says that it is good with a layered lamb stew.
